Untitled Project
GHOST Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Requirements
Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    This document defines the requirements for the GHOST prompt-preparation capability from receipt of raw user text until the prepared SuperPrompt is handed to the Knowledge Management and Memory Management retrieval paths.

    The capability has three major functional parts:
    1. deterministic prompt parsing, canonical field mapping, mandatory-field handling, and initial SuperPrompt construction;
    2. ActiveBrief-aware prompt materiality and topic-relation classification followed by deterministic routing policy;
    3. A2 PromptShaper classification of system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence policy.

    This document defines requirements. It does not serve as an implementation-status report.
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor UID: GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-FUT-ROOT GHOST Future Vision and Lifecycle Synchronization Scope
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      • GHOST-AGENT-STACK-ROOT Agent Stack Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      • GHOST-KM-ROOT Knowledge Management Scope
      • GHOST-DOC-INGEST-ROOT Document Ingestion Scope
      • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
      • GHOST-EVIDENCE-ROOT Evidence Selection and Condensation Scope
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT Memory Recording and Files Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
      • GHOST-CIPA-ROOT GHOST Context Integration and Prompt Assembly Scope
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Human Workbench and Interface Scope
      • GHOST-RUN-ROOT GHOST Runtime Platform and Operations Scope
      • GHOST-QGO-ROOT GHOST Quality, Governance, and Observability Scope
      STATEMENT: This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
    1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
    STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

    The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME
      1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

      The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in current source code and implementation paths.

      Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL
      1.3. Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: The GHOST prompt-preparation capability shall be organized into these three functional parts:

      1. Deterministic Prompt Structuring.
      2. ActiveBrief Relation Classification and Routing.
      3. A2 PromptShaper and Job Structuring.

      Each part shall have explicit inputs, outputs, responsibility boundaries, and acceptance criteria.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE
      1.4. Current Executable Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: The current executable scope shall include:
      - schema-driven deterministic header parsing and name resolution;
      - TASK and CONTEXT fallback behavior;
      - default handling for active mandatory fields;
      - SuperPrompt body and lifecycle updates;
      - ActiveBrief discovery and snapshotting;
      - two-dimensional LLM classification of prompt materiality and topic relation;
      - deterministic routing policy derived from the classifier result;
      - A2 selector execution or deterministic-default mode;
      - synchronized effective retrieval-query creation;
      - central SuperPrompt projection for the prepared prompt preview.

      Unimplemented semantic header matching, fuzzy matching, future A2 passes, and downstream retrieval algorithms shall not be presented as current behavior.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt State Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY Controller Coordination Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY Knowledge Management Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY Memory Management Boundary
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
      STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE AC: Whitespace-Only Input
      STATEMENT: The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION Initial Retrieval-Query Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Preprocessed Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION No Retrieval Artifact Mutation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic Intermediate Preview
      STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL
      6.1. Optional ActiveBrief Input UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-ELIGIBILITY Current Eligible ActiveBrief
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF-BYPASS No-ActiveBrief Classifier Bypass
      STATEMENT: Prompt preparation shall support execution with or without a configured memory manager and with or without an eligible ActiveBrief.

      Absence of an ActiveBrief shall be a valid routing state and shall not be treated as an LLM failure.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
      7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATERIALITY-CONTRACT Prompt Materiality Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CONTRACT Topic Relation Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFICATION-ORDER Classification Decision Order
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CONFIG Configured Relation Classifier
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RELATION-STATE Combined Relation State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
      STATEMENT: The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
      - prompt_materiality; and
      - topic_relation.

      The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
      8.1. A2 Input State UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD A2 Runtime Payload
      STATEMENT: A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

      Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR
      10.1. Prompt Schema Failure UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: A missing, unreadable, malformed, or non-object prompt schema shall fail explicitly during schema initialization.

      GHOST shall not silently continue with an invented schema when deterministic preprocessing depends on that configuration.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR
      10.2. Relation Classifier Failure Integrity UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: A provider failure, parse failure, missing required binding, or invalid classifier value shall not produce a fabricated STRONG, WEAK, SAME_TOPIC, RELATED_DOMAIN, or IRRELEVANT result.

      Fallback behavior, if introduced later, shall require an explicit approved requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR
      10.3. A2 Failure Integrity UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: A2 provider or parse failure shall not be represented as a successful LLM selection.

      The deterministic-default mode shall be used only when explicitly selected by the caller, not as an undisclosed replacement for a failed LLM call.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL
      10.4. No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall not invoke the relation-classifier LLM when no eligible ActiveBrief exists or when the classifier current-prompt text is empty.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY
      10.5. Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: Prompt preparation shall emit compact public lifecycle and relation summaries and may emit richer internal or developer diagnostics according to the logging policy.

      Raw prompt payloads, ActiveBrief bodies, model messages, and raw model outputs shall not be exposed through public logs by default.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY
      10.6. Deterministic Reproducibility UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: Given the same raw prompt, prompt schema, starting relevant SuperPrompt body state, and no LLM-enabled step, deterministic preprocessing shall produce the same recognized field values and routing-independent intermediate query text.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY
      10.7. Prompt Function Testability UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: Prompt parsing, name resolution, schema-to-runtime mapping, TASK fallback, routing-table construction, query projection, A2 sanitization, and SuperPrompt write-back shall be testable independently of the GUI.

      LLM-enabled classifier and A2 tests shall support provider stubs or recorded structured responses so deterministic post-processing can be verified.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS
      10.8. No Hidden Prompt Heuristics UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: Current prompt-preparation behavior shall not depend on undocumented fuzzy matching, semantic thresholds, hidden prompt repairs, or implicit ActiveBrief inclusion rules.

      Every current deterministic action shall be traceable to an explicit requirement and testable rule.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
      11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION Requirement and Implementation Synchronization
      STATEMENT: Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

      A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP
      11.2. Sibling Requirement Ownership UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed behavior shall remain in the following sibling requirement areas:

      - neutral AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient infrastructure in 20_agent_stack.sdoc;
      - runtime stage coordination in 10_orchestrator_and_pipeline.sdoc;
      - document ingestion in 41_document_ingestion.sdoc;
      - document retrieval and reranking in 42_document_retrieval_and_reranking.sdoc;
      - document usefulness selection and condensation in 43_evidence_selection_and_condensation.sdoc;
      - memory recording and ActiveBrief creation/update in 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc;
      - memory ingestion and retrieval in 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc;
      - memory merge, ActiveBrief continuity, and Blackboard direction in 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc;
      - current Streamlit controls and preview rendering in 61_streamlit_workbench_current.sdoc;
      - logging in 81_logging_textforge_raglog.sdoc;
      - security and traceability governance in 83_quality_security_traceability_governance.sdoc.

      This file shall not duplicate those subsystem algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY
      11.4. Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      STATEMENT: The current requirement baseline shall not present the following as implemented unless corresponding code and tests exist:
      - fuzzy or edit-distance header matching;
      - semantic or LLM-based header recovery;
      - template or keyword-cue field inference;
      - automatic multi-pass A2 refinement;
      - new prompt body fields without SuperPrompt support;
      - new relation labels or routing states;
      - retrieval or memory algorithms inside preprocessing.

      Such capabilities shall be introduced through explicit future requirements and controlled implementation changes.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

    The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in current source code and implementation paths.

    Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL
    1.3. Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: The GHOST prompt-preparation capability shall be organized into these three functional parts:

    1. Deterministic Prompt Structuring.
    2. ActiveBrief Relation Classification and Routing.
    3. A2 PromptShaper and Job Structuring.

    Each part shall have explicit inputs, outputs, responsibility boundaries, and acceptance criteria.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE
    1.4. Current Executable Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: The current executable scope shall include:
    - schema-driven deterministic header parsing and name resolution;
    - TASK and CONTEXT fallback behavior;
    - default handling for active mandatory fields;
    - SuperPrompt body and lifecycle updates;
    - ActiveBrief discovery and snapshotting;
    - two-dimensional LLM classification of prompt materiality and topic relation;
    - deterministic routing policy derived from the classifier result;
    - A2 selector execution or deterministic-default mode;
    - synchronized effective retrieval-query creation;
    - central SuperPrompt projection for the prepared prompt preview.

    Unimplemented semantic header matching, fuzzy matching, future A2 passes, and downstream retrieval algorithms shall not be presented as current behavior.

    2. 2. Functional Architecture and Responsibility Boundaries
    The prompt-preparation path combines deterministic processing and narrowly bounded LLM classification.

    The high-level flow is:

    raw user text
    -> deterministic parsing and canonicalization
    -> initial SuperPrompt update
    -> ActiveBrief relation classification
    -> deterministic six-state routing policy
    -> synchronized retrieval-query projection
    -> A2 PromptShaper
    -> prepared handoff to Knowledge and Memory Retrieval
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
    2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt State Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY Controller Coordination Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY Knowledge Management Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY Memory Management Boundary
    STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

    The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY
      2.2. Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: Header parsing, key normalization, canonical-name matching, alias matching, bilingual matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, active mandatory-field defaulting, body write-back, and lifecycle bookkeeping shall be deterministic.

      The deterministic preprocessing layer shall not call an LLM and shall not apply fuzzy, semantic, template, cue-based, or probabilistic header inference in the current runtime.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY
      2.3. ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: The ActiveBrief relation classifier shall decide only:
      - prompt_materiality; and
      - topic_relation.

      It shall not decide document-retrieval actions, memory-context actions, final prompt-display actions, pipeline stage order, or downstream evidence behavior.

      Those actions shall be determined by explicit deterministic Python policy.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY
      2.4. A2 PromptShaper Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: A2 PromptShaper shall classify configured prompt meta-fields from the already normalized current request.

      A2 shall not parse raw Markdown headers, select an ActiveBrief, execute Knowledge Retrieval, execute Memory Retrieval, or own final answer generation.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE
      2.5. Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: ActiveBrief relation classification and A2 PromptShaper shall use the neutral Agent Stack contracts defined in 20_agent_stack.sdoc.

      This file shall own the functional meaning, runtime payload, deterministic validation, routing policy, and SuperPrompt write-back of these concrete prompt functions.

      This file shall not duplicate the generic AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, or LLMClient infrastructure requirements.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY
      2.6. SuperPrompt State Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: SuperPrompt shall remain the authoritative shared runtime state for prompt preparation.

      PromptSchema, NameMatcher, ActiveBrief classifier configuration, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient shall not become parallel owners of the current prepared prompt state.

      All persistent prompt-preparation outcomes required by downstream stages shall be written explicitly into SuperPrompt body fields, lifecycle fields, or extras.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY
      2.7. Controller Coordination Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: The application controller shall coordinate deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, central prompt projection, and A2 invocation.

      The controller shall not absorb schema normalization rules, classifier semantics, A2 selector sanitization, or downstream retrieval algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY
      2.8. Knowledge Management Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: This file shall prepare the effective document-retrieval query and document-retrieval routing state.

      Detailed document ingestion, dense retrieval, SPLADE retrieval, fusion, reranking, usefulness selection, and evidence condensation shall remain owned by the Knowledge Management requirement files.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY
      2.9. Memory Management Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      STATEMENT: This file shall prepare the memory-context permission and ActiveBrief continuity state required by downstream memory processing.

      Detailed memory recording, ActiveBrief creation and update, memory ingestion, memory retrieval, memory scoring, and memory-context synthesis shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirement files.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt State Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY Controller Coordination Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY Knowledge Management Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY Memory Management Boundary
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY

    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
    STATEMENT: Header parsing, key normalization, canonical-name matching, alias matching, bilingual matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, active mandatory-field defaulting, body write-back, and lifecycle bookkeeping shall be deterministic.

    The deterministic preprocessing layer shall not call an LLM and shall not apply fuzzy, semantic, template, cue-based, or probabilistic header inference in the current runtime.

    - prompt_materiality; and
    - topic_relation.

    It shall not decide document-retrieval actions, memory-context actions, final prompt-display actions, pipeline stage order, or downstream evidence behavior.

    A2 shall not parse raw Markdown headers, select an ActiveBrief, execute Knowledge Retrieval, execute Memory Retrieval, or own final answer generation.
