    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY
    2.6. SuperPrompt State Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
    STATEMENT: SuperPrompt shall remain the authoritative shared runtime state for prompt preparation.

    PromptSchema, NameMatcher, ActiveBrief classifier configuration, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient shall not become parallel owners of the current prepared prompt state.

    All persistent prompt-preparation outcomes required by downstream stages shall be written explicitly into SuperPrompt body fields, lifecycle fields, or extras.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt State Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY Controller Coordination Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY Knowledge Management Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY Memory Management Boundary
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY
    2.7. Controller Coordination Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
    STATEMENT: The application controller shall coordinate deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, central prompt projection, and A2 invocation.

    The controller shall not absorb schema normalization rules, classifier semantics, A2 selector sanitization, or downstream retrieval algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt State Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY Controller Coordination Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY Knowledge Management Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY Memory Management Boundary
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY
    2.8. Knowledge Management Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
    STATEMENT: This file shall prepare the effective document-retrieval query and document-retrieval routing state.

    Detailed document ingestion, dense retrieval, SPLADE retrieval, fusion, reranking, usefulness selection, and evidence condensation shall remain owned by the Knowledge Management requirement files.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt State Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY Controller Coordination Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY Knowledge Management Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY Memory Management Boundary
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY
    2.9. Memory Management Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
    STATEMENT: This file shall prepare the memory-context permission and ActiveBrief continuity state required by downstream memory processing.

    Detailed memory recording, ActiveBrief creation and update, memory ingestion, memory retrieval, memory scoring, and memory-context synthesis shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirement files.

    3. 3. Prompt Schema and Canonical Field Contract
    Deterministic preprocessing is driven by a prompt schema. The schema defines the recognized prompt vocabulary; SuperPrompt defines the active runtime field model.

    Schema declarations and runtime fields shall remain explicitly mapped so that configured mandatory fields are not silently discarded.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
    3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
    STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

    The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON
      3.2. Prompt Schema JSON Contract UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      STATEMENT: The current prompt schema shall be represented as a JSON object.

      The executable deterministic preprocessing contract may use:
      - canonical_keys;
      - must_keys;
      - defaults;
      - aliases; and
      - bilingual mappings.

      Additional schema sections shall not be treated as active runtime behavior unless a current requirement and implementation consume them.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION
      3.3. Header-Key Normalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION AC: Header-Key Normalization
      STATEMENT: Prompt header keys shall be normalized deterministically by:
      1. accepting string input only;
      2. applying Unicode NFKC normalization;
      3. converting to lowercase;
      4. trimming leading and trailing whitespace;
      5. collapsing internal whitespace runs; and
      6. removing punctuation from the header key used for matching.

      Header-key normalization shall not modify the section body text.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
      3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
      STATEMENT: The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
      - system;
      - task;
      - audience;
      - role;
      - tone;
      - depth;
      - confidence;
      - evidence_policy;
      - context;
      - purpose;
      - format;
      - text.

      Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
      3.7. Canonical Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER AC: Canonical Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION
      3.12. Prompt Schema Version Traceability UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      STATEMENT: The prompt schema shall expose a version identifier sufficient to trace the canonical field vocabulary used by a prompt-preparation baseline.

      An approved change to canonical keys, mandatory fields, defaults, aliases, bilingual mappings, or schema-to-runtime mappings shall update the affected requirements and verification assets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
      STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON
    3.2. Prompt Schema JSON Contract UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
    STATEMENT: The current prompt schema shall be represented as a JSON object.

    The executable deterministic preprocessing contract may use:
    - canonical_keys;
    - must_keys;
    - defaults;
    - aliases; and
    - bilingual mappings.

    Additional schema sections shall not be treated as active runtime behavior unless a current requirement and implementation consume them.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
      STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION
    3.3. Header-Key Normalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION AC: Header-Key Normalization
    STATEMENT: Prompt header keys shall be normalized deterministically by:
    1. accepting string input only;
    2. applying Unicode NFKC normalization;
    3. converting to lowercase;
    4. trimming leading and trailing whitespace;
    5. collapsing internal whitespace runs; and
    6. removing punctuation from the header key used for matching.

    Header-key normalization shall not modify the section body text.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION
      12.6. AC: Header-Key Normalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      STATEMENT: Given two header names that differ only by case, Unicode compatibility form, outer whitespace, internal whitespace runs, or punctuation removed by the normalization contract, they shall resolve to the same normalized matching key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
      STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
    3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
    STATEMENT: The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
    - system;
    - task;
    - audience;
    - role;
    - tone;
    - depth;
    - confidence;
    - evidence_policy;
    - context;
    - purpose;
    - format;
    - text.

    Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
      3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Mandatory Field Runtime Destination
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping
      STATEMENT: Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

      In particular:
      - response_depth shall map to depth;
      - output_format shall map to format.

      A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
      3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
      STATEMENT: The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
      - system;
      - task;
      - audience;
      - role;
      - tone;
      - depth;
      - confidence;
      - evidence_policy;
      - context;
      - purpose;
      - format;
      - text.

      Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
    3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Mandatory Field Runtime Destination
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping
    STATEMENT: Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

    In particular:
    - response_depth shall map to depth;
    - output_format shall map to format.

    A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION
      3.6. Mandatory Field Runtime Destination UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS Other Mandatory Field Defaults
      STATEMENT: Every active schema field declared mandatory shall have one defined runtime destination or shall be removed from the active mandatory-field set.

      The system shall not silently claim enforcement of a mandatory schema field whose value is discarded before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP
      12.11. AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
      STATEMENT: Given a recognized response_depth or output_format value, prompt preprocessing shall route it to runtime depth or format respectively.

      A configured mandatory field without a runtime destination shall fail requirement conformance rather than being reported as enforced.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
      3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Mandatory Field Runtime Destination
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping
      STATEMENT: Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

      In particular:
      - response_depth shall map to depth;
      - output_format shall map to format.

      A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION
    3.6. Mandatory Field Runtime Destination UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS Other Mandatory Field Defaults
    STATEMENT: Every active schema field declared mandatory shall have one defined runtime destination or shall be removed from the active mandatory-field set.

    The system shall not silently claim enforcement of a mandatory schema field whose value is discarded before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS
      4.12. Other Mandatory Field Defaults UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Mandatory Field Runtime Destination
      STATEMENT: After TASK fallback, each other active mandatory prompt field that remains absent or empty shall receive its configured schema default.

      Defaults shall be applied only when the field has an active runtime destination.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
      STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
    3.7. Canonical Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER AC: Canonical Header Resolution
    STATEMENT: A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
      3.8. Alias Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER AC: Alias Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

      The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER
      12.3. AC: Canonical Header Resolution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      STATEMENT: Given a supported heading named TASK, the parser shall resolve it canonically and write its body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
      3.7. Canonical Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER AC: Canonical Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
    3.8. Alias Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER AC: Alias Header Resolution
    STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

    The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
      3.9. Bilingual Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Deterministic Name-Matching Order
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER AC: Bilingual Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

      The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER
      12.4. AC: Alias Header Resolution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      STATEMENT: Given a heading named Objective and an alias mapping from objective to purpose, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime purpose field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
      3.8. Alias Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER AC: Alias Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

      The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
    3.9. Bilingual Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Deterministic Name-Matching Order
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER AC: Bilingual Header Resolution
    STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

    The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
      3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching
      STATEMENT: Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
      1. canonical match;
      2. alias match;
      3. bilingual match;
      4. unknown.

      A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER
      12.5. AC: Bilingual Header Resolution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
      STATEMENT: Given a heading named Aufgabe and a configured German mapping from aufgabe to task, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
      3.9. Bilingual Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Deterministic Name-Matching Order
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER AC: Bilingual Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

      The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
    3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching
    STATEMENT: Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
    1. canonical match;
    2. alias match;
    3. bilingual match;
    4. unknown.

    A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
      3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Deterministic Name-Matching Order
      STATEMENT: The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

      Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
      3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching
      STATEMENT: Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
      1. canonical match;
      2. alias match;
      3. bilingual match;
      4. unknown.

      A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
    3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Deterministic Name-Matching Order
    STATEMENT: The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

    Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON Prompt Schema JSON Contract
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION Prompt Schema Version Traceability
      STATEMENT: The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION
    3.12. Prompt Schema Version Traceability UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
    STATEMENT: The prompt schema shall expose a version identifier sufficient to trace the canonical field vocabulary used by a prompt-preparation baseline.

    An approved change to canonical keys, mandatory fields, defaults, aliases, bilingual mappings, or schema-to-runtime mappings shall update the affected requirements and verification assets.

    4. 4. Deterministic Prompt Parsing and Name Resolution
    The deterministic parser converts either Markdown-like structured input or unstructured plain text into recognized prompt sections.

    The parser does not infer user intent beyond the explicit deterministic rules in this section.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
    4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE AC: Whitespace-Only Input
    STATEMENT: The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

    Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
      4.2. Supported Header Levels UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX Current Header Syntax
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY Section Body Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      STATEMENT: Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

      Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE
      4.5. Plain-Text Prompt Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK
      STATEMENT: When no supported heading is present, the complete trimmed raw user text shall become the TASK value.

      Plain-text mode shall not require schema name matching.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE
      12.1. AC: Whitespace-Only Input UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      STATEMENT: Given whitespace-only raw input, the controller shall return the existing SuperPrompt without invoking deterministic preprocessing, relation classification, or A2 and without appending a new preprocessing history entry.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE AC: Whitespace-Only Input
      STATEMENT: The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
    4.2. Supported Header Levels UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX Current Header Syntax
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY Section Body Boundary
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
    STATEMENT: Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

    Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX
      4.3. Current Header Syntax UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      STATEMENT: A current prompt-field heading shall contain:
      - one to three leading hash characters;
      - optional whitespace after the hash characters; and
      - a non-empty header title.

      Therefore both "## TASK" and "##TASK" shall be interpreted according to the same current header-title matching rules.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY
      4.4. Section Body Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      STATEMENT: The body of a recognized or unrecognized heading shall extend from the end of that heading to the beginning of the next supported heading or to the end of the raw input.

      Leading and trailing whitespace around each extracted body shall be removed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Prompt Body Text Preservation
      STATEMENT: For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
      4.2. Supported Header Levels UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX Current Header Syntax
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY Section Body Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      STATEMENT: Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

      Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX
    4.3. Current Header Syntax UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
    STATEMENT: A current prompt-field heading shall contain:
    - one to three leading hash characters;
    - optional whitespace after the hash characters; and
    - a non-empty header title.

    Therefore both "## TASK" and "##TASK" shall be interpreted according to the same current header-title matching rules.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
      4.2. Supported Header Levels UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX Current Header Syntax
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY Section Body Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      STATEMENT: Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

      Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY
    4.4. Section Body Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
    STATEMENT: The body of a recognized or unrecognized heading shall extend from the end of that heading to the beginning of the next supported heading or to the end of the raw input.

    Leading and trailing whitespace around each extracted body shall be removed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE AC: Whitespace-Only Input
      STATEMENT: The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE
    4.5. Plain-Text Prompt Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK
    STATEMENT: When no supported heading is present, the complete trimmed raw user text shall become the TASK value.

    Plain-text mode shall not require schema name matching.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK
      12.2. AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
      STATEMENT: Given raw input with no supported heading, deterministic preprocessing shall write the complete trimmed input into SuperPrompt.body["task"], set stage to preprocessed, append the preprocessing history entry, and preserve retrieval artifacts.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
      4.2. Supported Header Levels UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX Current Header Syntax
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY Section Body Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      STATEMENT: Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

      Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
    4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Prompt Body Text Preservation
    STATEMENT: For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
      4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS AC: Repeated Field Last Wins
      STATEMENT: When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

      The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
      4.8. Unknown Header Handling UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER AC: Unknown Header Isolation
      STATEMENT: A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

      Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

      The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
      4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK TASK from CONTEXT Fallback
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback
      STATEMENT: Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

      Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
      4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      STATEMENT: Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

      Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Prompt Body Text Preservation
      STATEMENT: For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
    4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS AC: Repeated Field Last Wins
    STATEMENT: When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

    The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
      12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
      STATEMENT: Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Prompt Body Text Preservation
      STATEMENT: For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
    4.8. Unknown Header Handling UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER AC: Unknown Header Isolation
    STATEMENT: A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

    Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

    The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
      12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
      STATEMENT: Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Prompt Body Text Preservation
      STATEMENT: For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
    4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK TASK from CONTEXT Fallback
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback
    STATEMENT: Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

    Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
      4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK
      STATEMENT: If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

      The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
      4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback
      STATEMENT: If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
      4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK TASK from CONTEXT Fallback
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback
      STATEMENT: Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

      Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
    4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK
    STATEMENT: If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

    The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
      12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK TASK from CONTEXT Fallback
      STATEMENT: Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
      4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK TASK from CONTEXT Fallback
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback
      STATEMENT: Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

      Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
    4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback
    STATEMENT: If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
      12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback
      STATEMENT: Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION
      3.6. Mandatory Field Runtime Destination UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS Other Mandatory Field Defaults
      STATEMENT: Every active schema field declared mandatory shall have one defined runtime destination or shall be removed from the active mandatory-field set.

      The system shall not silently claim enforcement of a mandatory schema field whose value is discarded before SuperPrompt write-back.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS
    4.12. Other Mandatory Field Defaults UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Mandatory Field Runtime Destination
    STATEMENT: After TASK fallback, each other active mandatory prompt field that remains absent or empty shall receive its configured schema default.

    Defaults shall be applied only when the field has an active runtime destination.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Prompt Body Text Preservation
      STATEMENT: For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
    4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
    STATEMENT: Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

    Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.

    5. 5. SuperPrompt Construction and Initial Projection
    Deterministic preprocessing updates the existing SuperPrompt in place. It establishes the normalized current request before ActiveBrief routing and A2 shaping occur.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
    5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION Initial Retrieval-Query Projection
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Preprocessed Stage Mark
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION No Retrieval Artifact Mutation
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic Intermediate Preview
    STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

    It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
      5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS No Dynamic Body-Field Creation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS Preservation of Untouched Fields
      STATEMENT: Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

      The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION
      5.5. Initial Retrieval-Query Projection UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      STATEMENT: After deterministic body write-back, GHOST may build an initial retrieval-oriented text projection from TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT.

      This initial projection shall not be treated as final until ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing have completed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
      5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Preprocessing History Entry
      STATEMENT: Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
      5.8. No Retrieval Artifact Mutation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      STATEMENT: Prompt preparation before the retrieval handoff shall not create, rank, remove, or rewrite document chunks, memory candidates, stage ranking views, final document selections, condensed document context, or synthesized memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW
      5.9. Deterministic Intermediate Preview UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION Central Prompt Projection after Preparation
      STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing may create an intermediate prompt preview from the currently populated prompt fields.

      That intermediate preview shall not supersede the final centralized projection performed after relation classification and A2 shaping.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION Initial Retrieval-Query Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Preprocessed Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION No Retrieval Artifact Mutation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic Intermediate Preview
      STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
    5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS No Dynamic Body-Field Creation
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS Preservation of Untouched Fields
    STATEMENT: Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

    The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS
      5.3. No Dynamic Body-Field Creation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
      STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall not add new keys outside the active SuperPrompt body contract.

      New canonical prompt fields shall require an approved SuperPrompt contract change and explicit schema-to-runtime mapping.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS
      5.4. Preservation of Untouched Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
      STATEMENT: A body field not recognized, defaulted, or explicitly cleared by the current preprocessing rules shall retain its existing value.

      Prompt preparation shall not clear retrieval artifacts or unrelated SuperPrompt fields merely because preprocessing is re-run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
      5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS No Dynamic Body-Field Creation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS Preservation of Untouched Fields
      STATEMENT: Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

      The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS
    5.3. No Dynamic Body-Field Creation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
    STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall not add new keys outside the active SuperPrompt body contract.

    New canonical prompt fields shall require an approved SuperPrompt contract change and explicit schema-to-runtime mapping.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
      5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS No Dynamic Body-Field Creation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS Preservation of Untouched Fields
      STATEMENT: Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

      The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS
    5.4. Preservation of Untouched Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
    STATEMENT: A body field not recognized, defaulted, or explicitly cleared by the current preprocessing rules shall retain its existing value.

    Prompt preparation shall not clear retrieval artifacts or unrelated SuperPrompt fields merely because preprocessing is re-run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Canonical Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION Initial Retrieval-Query Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Preprocessed Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION No Retrieval Artifact Mutation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic Intermediate Preview
      STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION
    5.5. Initial Retrieval-Query Projection UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
    STATEMENT: After deterministic body write-back, GHOST may build an initial retrieval-oriented text projection from TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT.

    This initial projection shall not be treated as final until ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing have completed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION Initial Retrieval-Query Projection

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION No Retrieval Artifact Mutation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic Intermediate Preview
      STATEMENT: Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
    5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Preprocessing History Entry
    STATEMENT: Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK
      5.7. Preprocessing History Entry UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Preprocessed Stage Mark
      STATEMENT: Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall append "preprocessed" to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
      5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Preprocessing History Entry
      STATEMENT: Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK
    5.7. Preprocessing History Entry UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Preprocessed Stage Mark
    STATEMENT: Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall append "preprocessed" to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION No Retrieval Artifact Mutation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic Intermediate Preview

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.

    STATEMENT: Prompt preparation before the retrieval handoff shall not create, rank, remove, or rewrite document chunks, memory candidates, stage ranking views, final document selections, condensed document context, or synthesized memory context.
