      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX AC: Six-State Deterministic Routing Matrix
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
      - prompt_materiality;
      - topic_relation;
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
      - final_prompt_mode;
      - allow_memory_context; and
      - memory_context_policy.

      The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-LOG
    7.20. Public Relation Summary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-LOG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
    STATEMENT: Prompt preparation shall emit a compact user-visible summary of the current topic relation and prompt strength after the routing result is written.

    Detailed prompts, ActiveBrief bodies, or raw model output shall not be exposed through the public log solely to explain the classification.

    8. 8. A2 PromptShaper and Job Structuring
    A2 PromptShaper refines the prepared request into explicit meta-configuration for downstream answering and evidence handling.

    A2 is a concrete selector consumer of the neutral Agent Stack. Its functional semantics and deterministic safety layer are defined here.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
    8.1. A2 Input State UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD A2 Runtime Payload
    STATEMENT: A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

    Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS A2 Decision Targets
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 Configured Model Settings
      STATEMENT: A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD
      8.4. A2 Runtime Payload UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      STATEMENT: A2 shall build its prompt-under-evaluation from the available PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT values of the current SuperPrompt.

      A2 shall also provide an explicit required-output JSON shape derived from the active configured decision targets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
      8.1. A2 Input State UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD A2 Runtime Payload
      STATEMENT: A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

      Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
    8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS A2 Decision Targets
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 Configured Model Settings
    STATEMENT: A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

    The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS
      8.3. A2 Decision Targets UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      STATEMENT: The current A2 PromptShaper shall classify these six fields:
      - system;
      - audience;
      - tone;
      - depth;
      - confidence;
      - evidence_policy.

      System may select one to three configured role identifiers. Each other field shall select exactly one configured identifier.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
      8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      STATEMENT: When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
      1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
      2. compose the selector messages;
      3. call the configured LLM path;
      4. parse the structured result; and
      5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE
      8.6. A2 Deterministic-Default Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      STATEMENT: When A2 LLM mode is disabled, A2 shall construct its candidate selector result deterministically from configured defaults.

      If a configured default is absent, A2 may use the first allowed configured option or the allowed prefix required by a multi-selection target.

      The same deterministic sanitization and write-back rules shall apply after candidate construction.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS
      8.7. A2 Configured Model Settings UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      STATEMENT: A2 shall use model name, temperature where applicable, and maximum output-token allowance obtained from its AgentPrompt configuration.

      A2 business logic shall not silently replace configured model settings with unrelated hard-coded values.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS A2 Decision Targets
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 Configured Model Settings
      STATEMENT: A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS
    8.3. A2 Decision Targets UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
    STATEMENT: The current A2 PromptShaper shall classify these six fields:
    - system;
    - audience;
    - tone;
    - depth;
    - confidence;
    - evidence_policy.

    System may select one to three configured role identifiers. Each other field shall select exactly one configured identifier.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
      8.1. A2 Input State UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD A2 Runtime Payload
      STATEMENT: A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

      Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD
    8.4. A2 Runtime Payload UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
    STATEMENT: A2 shall build its prompt-under-evaluation from the available PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT values of the current SuperPrompt.

    A2 shall also provide an explicit required-output JSON shape derived from the active configured decision targets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS A2 Decision Targets
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 Configured Model Settings
      STATEMENT: A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
    8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
    STATEMENT: When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
    1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
    2. compose the selector messages;
    3. call the configured LLM path;
    4. parse the structured result; and
    5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS A2 Decision Targets
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 Configured Model Settings
      STATEMENT: A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE
    8.6. A2 Deterministic-Default Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
    STATEMENT: When A2 LLM mode is disabled, A2 shall construct its candidate selector result deterministically from configured defaults.

    If a configured default is absent, A2 may use the first allowed configured option or the allowed prefix required by a multi-selection target.

    The same deterministic sanitization and write-back rules shall apply after candidate construction.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT
      12.23. AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      STATEMENT: Given use_llm = false and valid configured defaults, A2 shall produce a sanitized selector result without an LLM call, mark a2_llm_used false, and mark its mode as deterministic_defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS A2 Decision Targets
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 Configured Model Settings
      STATEMENT: A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS
    8.7. A2 Configured Model Settings UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
    STATEMENT: A2 shall use model name, temperature where applicable, and maximum output-token allowance obtained from its AgentPrompt configuration.

    A2 business logic shall not silently replace configured model settings with unrelated hard-coded values.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
      8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      STATEMENT: When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
      1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
      2. compose the selector messages;
      3. call the configured LLM path;
      4. parse the structured result; and
      5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
    8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
    STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

    The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL
      8.9. A2 Invalid Identifier Removal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization
      STATEMENT: For each A2 field, deterministic sanitization shall remove option identifiers that are not present in that field's allowed catalog.

      This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL
      8.10. A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      STATEMENT: Deterministic A2 sanitization shall remove duplicate option identifiers while preserving the first valid occurrence order.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY
      8.11. A2 Cardinality Enforcement UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      STATEMENT: A2 sanitization shall enforce each active field's configured maximum selection count.

      When more valid identifiers are returned than allowed, the first valid identifiers up to the configured maximum shall be retained.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
      8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation
      STATEMENT: If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

      A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 Diagnostic Metadata
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back
      STATEMENT: A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL
    8.9. A2 Invalid Identifier Removal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization
    STATEMENT: For each A2 field, deterministic sanitization shall remove option identifiers that are not present in that field's allowed catalog.

    This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID
      12.21. AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      STATEMENT: Given an A2 result containing an invented identifier or an identifier from another field's catalog, the sanitizer shall remove that identifier before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL
    8.10. A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
    STATEMENT: Deterministic A2 sanitization shall remove duplicate option identifiers while preserving the first valid occurrence order.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY
    8.11. A2 Cardinality Enforcement UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
    STATEMENT: A2 sanitization shall enforce each active field's configured maximum selection count.

    When more valid identifiers are returned than allowed, the first valid identifiers up to the configured maximum shall be retained.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
    8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation
    STATEMENT: If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

    A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE
      12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      STATEMENT: Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE A2 LLM Selector Mode
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 Cardinality Enforcement
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
    8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 Diagnostic Metadata
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back
    STATEMENT: A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

    When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

    For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS
      8.14. A2 Diagnostic Metadata UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: A2 shall write stable runtime metadata sufficient to identify:
      - the sanitized selected identifiers;
      - whether the LLM was used; and
      - whether execution used LLM mode or deterministic-default mode.

      Current keys shall include a2_selected_ids, a2_llm_used, and a2_mode.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION A2 Prompt Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 Retrieval-State Preservation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN Safe A2 Re-Execution
      STATEMENT: A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL
      12.24. AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      STATEMENT: Given valid sanitized A2 identifiers with configured labels, SuperPrompt extras shall retain the identifiers while the body fields contain their human-readable labels.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 Diagnostic Metadata
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back
      STATEMENT: A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS
    8.14. A2 Diagnostic Metadata UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
    STATEMENT: A2 shall write stable runtime metadata sufficient to identify:
    - the sanitized selected identifiers;
    - whether the LLM was used; and
    - whether execution used LLM mode or deterministic-default mode.

    Current keys shall include a2_selected_ids, a2_llm_used, and a2_mode.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 Diagnostic Metadata
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back
      STATEMENT: A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
    8.15. A2 Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION A2 Prompt Projection
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 Retrieval-State Preservation
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN Safe A2 Re-Execution
    STATEMENT: A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

    A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
      8.16. A2 Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY A2 History Entry
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation
      STATEMENT: After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION
      8.18. A2 Prompt Projection UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      STATEMENT: After A2 write-back, GHOST shall rebuild prompt_ready through the current central SuperPrompt composition contract so that selected meta-field labels and evidence policy are visible in the prepared prompt preview.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
      8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      STATEMENT: A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN
      8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      STATEMENT: The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

      Re-running A2 shall apply the same sanitization and write-back rules and shall not clear existing retrieval artifacts solely because the lifecycle stage returns to "a2".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION A2 Prompt Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 Retrieval-State Preservation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN Safe A2 Re-Execution
      STATEMENT: A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
    8.16. A2 Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY A2 History Entry
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation
    STATEMENT: After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY
      8.17. A2 History Entry UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      STATEMENT: After successful A2 processing, "a2" shall be appended to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE
      12.25. AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      STATEMENT: After successful A2 execution, stage shall equal a2, history shall contain the new a2 entry, prompt_ready shall reflect the A2 selections, and existing document or memory retrieval artifacts shall remain unchanged.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
      8.16. A2 Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY A2 History Entry
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation
      STATEMENT: After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY
    8.17. A2 History Entry UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
    STATEMENT: After successful A2 processing, "a2" shall be appended to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION A2 Prompt Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 Retrieval-State Preservation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN Safe A2 Re-Execution
      STATEMENT: A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION
    8.18. A2 Prompt Projection UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
    STATEMENT: After A2 write-back, GHOST shall rebuild prompt_ready through the current central SuperPrompt composition contract so that selected meta-field labels and evidence policy are visible in the prepared prompt preview.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION A2 Prompt Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 Retrieval-State Preservation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN Safe A2 Re-Execution
      STATEMENT: A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
    8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
    STATEMENT: A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION A2 Prompt Projection
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 Retrieval-State Preservation
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN Safe A2 Re-Execution
      STATEMENT: A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN
    8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
    STATEMENT: The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

    Re-running A2 shall apply the same sanitization and write-back rules and shall not clear existing retrieval artifacts solely because the lifecycle stage returns to "a2".

    9. 9. Prepared Handoff to Knowledge and Memory Retrieval
    The prompt-preparation capability finishes when the structured current request, routing policy, A2 meta-configuration, effective retrieval query, and prompt preview are mutually consistent.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
    9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
    STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

    The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE
      9.2. Retrieval Query Source Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      STATEMENT: The current prompt portion of the effective retrieval query shall be derived from available TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT values.

      A2 meta-fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence_policy shall not be inserted into the document-retrieval query merely because they appear in the final prompt preview.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF
      9.3. ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      STATEMENT: ActiveBrief content shall contribute to the effective retrieval query only according to the deterministic routing state:
      - WEAK + SAME_TOPIC: full ActiveBrief body;
      - WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN: ActiveBrief title;
      - all STRONG states: no ActiveBrief contribution;
      - WEAK + IRRELEVANT: empty query;
      - NO_ACTIVEBRIEF: current prompt only.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
      9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION No Knowledge Retrieval Execution
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
      - the prepared SuperPrompt;
      - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
      - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

      This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF
      9.5. Memory Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION No Memory Retrieval Execution
      STATEMENT: The Memory Management path shall receive:
      - the prepared SuperPrompt;
      - the ActiveBrief snapshot and relation state;
      - allow_memory_context; and
      - memory_context_policy.

      This handoff shall not precompute or synthesize memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL
      9.6. Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      STATEMENT: An empty effective document-retrieval query produced by WEAK + IRRELEVANT routing shall be treated as an explicit document-retrieval suppression signal.

      Downstream orchestration shall skip document retrieval or handle the state without attempting a normal retrieval call with an empty query.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
      9.7. Prepared-State Consistency UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff
      STATEMENT: At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
      - SuperPrompt body fields;
      - ActiveBrief relation extras;
      - deterministic routing decision;
      - effective retrieval-query text;
      - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
      - lifecycle stage and history;
      - prompt_ready.

      Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD
      12.19. AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      STATEMENT: Given an initial pre-classification query and a final routing state that changes ActiveBrief contribution or suppresses retrieval, the effective retrieval-query field shall be rebuilt after classification and shall reflect the final state at handoff.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE
    9.2. Retrieval Query Source Fields UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    STATEMENT: The current prompt portion of the effective retrieval query shall be derived from available TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT values.

    A2 meta-fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence_policy shall not be inserted into the document-retrieval query merely because they appear in the final prompt preview.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF
    9.3. ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    STATEMENT: ActiveBrief content shall contribute to the effective retrieval query only according to the deterministic routing state:
    - WEAK + SAME_TOPIC: full ActiveBrief body;
    - WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN: ActiveBrief title;
    - all STRONG states: no ActiveBrief contribution;
    - WEAK + IRRELEVANT: empty query;
    - NO_ACTIVEBRIEF: current prompt only.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
    9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION No Knowledge Retrieval Execution
    STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
    - the prepared SuperPrompt;
    - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
    - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

    This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION
      9.8. No Knowledge Retrieval Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall not query ChromaDB, SPLADE indexes, document manifests, rerankers, A3, or A4.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF
    9.5. Memory Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION No Memory Retrieval Execution
    STATEMENT: The Memory Management path shall receive:
    - the prepared SuperPrompt;
    - the ActiveBrief snapshot and relation state;
    - allow_memory_context; and
    - memory_context_policy.

    This handoff shall not precompute or synthesize memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION
      9.9. No Memory Retrieval Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall not query memory vector stores, run memory candidate scoring, merge memory records, or synthesize final memory context.

      Reading the current eligible ActiveBrief for relation classification shall not be treated as full Memory Retrieval execution.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL
    9.6. Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    STATEMENT: An empty effective document-retrieval query produced by WEAK + IRRELEVANT routing shall be treated as an explicit document-retrieval suppression signal.

    Downstream orchestration shall skip document retrieval or handle the state without attempting a normal retrieval call with an empty query.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
    9.7. Prepared-State Consistency UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff
    STATEMENT: At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
    - SuperPrompt body fields;
    - ActiveBrief relation extras;
    - deterministic routing decision;
    - effective retrieval-query text;
    - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
    - lifecycle stage and history;
    - prompt_ready.

    Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY
      9.10. Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      STATEMENT: The prepared prompt preview shall present the current user request as the highest-priority task.

      Any ActiveBrief continuity material shall be presented only as supporting context according to final_prompt_mode.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF
      12.26. AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      STATEMENT: At completion of prompt preparation, the SuperPrompt body, routing extras, effective retrieval query, A2 metadata, stage/history, and prompt_ready shall be mutually consistent and sufficient for Knowledge and Memory Retrieval to begin without reparsing the raw user prompt.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
      9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION No Knowledge Retrieval Execution
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
      - the prepared SuperPrompt;
      - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
      - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

      This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION
    9.8. No Knowledge Retrieval Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation
    STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall not query ChromaDB, SPLADE indexes, document manifests, rerankers, A3, or A4.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL
      12.27. AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION No Knowledge Retrieval Execution
      STATEMENT: Executing the complete prompt-preparation capability shall not query document or memory vector stores and shall not run reranking, A3, A4, or memory synthesis.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF
      9.5. Memory Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION No Memory Retrieval Execution
      STATEMENT: The Memory Management path shall receive:
      - the prepared SuperPrompt;
      - the ActiveBrief snapshot and relation state;
      - allow_memory_context; and
      - memory_context_policy.

      This handoff shall not precompute or synthesize memory context.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION
    9.9. No Memory Retrieval Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
    STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall not query memory vector stores, run memory candidate scoring, merge memory records, or synthesize final memory context.

    Reading the current eligible ActiveBrief for relation classification shall not be treated as full Memory Retrieval execution.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
      9.7. Prepared-State Consistency UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff
      STATEMENT: At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
      - SuperPrompt body fields;
      - ActiveBrief relation extras;
      - deterministic routing decision;
      - effective retrieval-query text;
      - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
      - lifecycle stage and history;
      - prompt_ready.

      Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY
    9.10. Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
    STATEMENT: The prepared prompt preview shall present the current user request as the highest-priority task.

    Any ActiveBrief continuity material shall be presented only as supporting context according to final_prompt_mode.

    10. 10. Error Handling, Logging, and Quality Requirements
    Prompt preparation combines deterministic logic with two bounded LLM-enabled functions. Failures shall remain explicit, observable, and traceable without fabricating successful routing or shaping results.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR
    10.1. Prompt Schema Failure UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: A missing, unreadable, malformed, or non-object prompt schema shall fail explicitly during schema initialization.

    GHOST shall not silently continue with an invented schema when deterministic preprocessing depends on that configuration.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR
    10.2. Relation Classifier Failure Integrity UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: A provider failure, parse failure, missing required binding, or invalid classifier value shall not produce a fabricated STRONG, WEAK, SAME_TOPIC, RELATED_DOMAIN, or IRRELEVANT result.

    Fallback behavior, if introduced later, shall require an explicit approved requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR
    10.3. A2 Failure Integrity UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: A2 provider or parse failure shall not be represented as a successful LLM selection.

    The deterministic-default mode shall be used only when explicitly selected by the caller, not as an undisclosed replacement for a failed LLM call.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):

      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL
    10.4. No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: GHOST shall not invoke the relation-classifier LLM when no eligible ActiveBrief exists or when the classifier current-prompt text is empty.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY
    10.5. Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: Prompt preparation shall emit compact public lifecycle and relation summaries and may emit richer internal or developer diagnostics according to the logging policy.

    Raw prompt payloads, ActiveBrief bodies, model messages, and raw model outputs shall not be exposed through public logs by default.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor

      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY
    10.6. Deterministic Reproducibility UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: Given the same raw prompt, prompt schema, starting relevant SuperPrompt body state, and no LLM-enabled step, deterministic preprocessing shall produce the same recognized field values and routing-independent intermediate query text.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
