      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY
    10.7. Prompt Function Testability UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: Prompt parsing, name resolution, schema-to-runtime mapping, TASK fallback, routing-table construction, query projection, A2 sanitization, and SuperPrompt write-back shall be testable independently of the GUI.

    LLM-enabled classifier and A2 tests shall support provider stubs or recorded structured responses so deterministic post-processing can be verified.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics

      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS
    10.8. No Hidden Prompt Heuristics UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: Current prompt-preparation behavior shall not depend on undocumented fuzzy matching, semantic thresholds, hidden prompt repairs, or implicit ActiveBrief inclusion rules.

    Every current deterministic action shall be traceable to an explicit requirement and testable rule.

    11. 11. Traceability and Sibling Requirement Ownership
    The current implementation trace anchors for this requirement area are:

    - ragstream/preprocessing/preprocessing.py
    - ragstream/preprocessing/prompt_schema.py
    - ragstream/preprocessing/name_matcher.py
    - ragstream/preprocessing/activebrief_relation_classifier.py
    - ragstream/config/prompt_schema.json
    - data/agents/activebrief_relation_classifier/activebrief_relation_classifier_001.json
    - ragstream/agents/a2_promptshaper.py
    - data/agents/a2_promptshaper/003.json
    - data/agents/a2_promptshaper/a2_catalogs/003_option_catalogs_system.json
    - data/agents/a2_promptshaper/a2_catalogs/003_option_catalogs_rest.json
    - ragstream/orchestration/super_prompt.py
    - ragstream/orchestration/superprompt_projector.py
    - ragstream/app/controller.py

    These are trace anchors, not a replacement for the normative requirements in this file.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
    11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION Requirement and Implementation Synchronization
    STATEMENT: Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

    A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION
      11.3. Requirement and Implementation Synchronization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization
      STATEMENT: An approved change to prompt schema semantics, deterministic parsing, canonical field mapping, ActiveBrief routing, A2 targets, query projection, public interfaces, or SuperPrompt write-back shall update the affected requirements and verification assets together with the implementation change.

      Private refactoring that preserves required behavior and interfaces shall not require new functional requirements.

      Code that contradicts an approved requirement in this file shall be treated as a defect or unapproved change until the contradiction is resolved.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP
    11.2. Sibling Requirement Ownership UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: Detailed behavior shall remain in the following sibling requirement areas:

    - neutral AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient infrastructure in 20_agent_stack.sdoc;
    - runtime stage coordination in 10_orchestrator_and_pipeline.sdoc;
    - document ingestion in 41_document_ingestion.sdoc;
    - document retrieval and reranking in 42_document_retrieval_and_reranking.sdoc;
    - document usefulness selection and condensation in 43_evidence_selection_and_condensation.sdoc;
    - memory recording and ActiveBrief creation/update in 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc;
    - memory ingestion and retrieval in 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc;
    - memory merge, ActiveBrief continuity, and Blackboard direction in 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc;
    - current Streamlit controls and preview rendering in 61_streamlit_workbench_current.sdoc;
    - logging in 81_logging_textforge_raglog.sdoc;
    - security and traceability governance in 83_quality_security_traceability_governance.sdoc.

    This file shall not duplicate those subsystem algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
      11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION Requirement and Implementation Synchronization
      STATEMENT: Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

      A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION
    11.3. Requirement and Implementation Synchronization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization
    STATEMENT: An approved change to prompt schema semantics, deterministic parsing, canonical field mapping, ActiveBrief routing, A2 targets, query projection, public interfaces, or SuperPrompt write-back shall update the affected requirements and verification assets together with the implementation change.

    Private refactoring that preserves required behavior and interfaces shall not require new functional requirements.

    Code that contradicts an approved requirement in this file shall be treated as a defect or unapproved change until the contradiction is resolved.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION
      12.28. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION Requirement and Implementation Synchronization
      STATEMENT: An implementation baseline shall not be accepted when its prompt-schema mapping, routing table, query projection, final prompt projection, or A2 sanitization contradicts an approved requirement in this file.

      The contradiction shall be resolved by correcting the defect or approving a coordinated requirement and implementation change.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE Current Executable Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE In-Place SuperPrompt Update
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Optional ActiveBrief Input
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 Input State
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR Prompt Schema Failure
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR Relation Classifier Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 Failure Integrity
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Deterministic Reproducibility
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt Function Testability
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS No Hidden Prompt Heuristics
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Sibling Requirement Ownership
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY
    11.4. Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
    STATEMENT: The current requirement baseline shall not present the following as implemented unless corresponding code and tests exist:
    - fuzzy or edit-distance header matching;
    - semantic or LLM-based header recovery;
    - template or keyword-cue field inference;
    - automatic multi-pass A2 refinement;
    - new prompt body fields without SuperPrompt support;
    - new relation labels or routing states;
    - retrieval or memory algorithms inside preprocessing.

    Such capabilities shall be introduced through explicit future requirements and controlled implementation changes.

    12. 12. Acceptance Criteria
    These acceptance criteria validate the complete prompt-preparation path without duplicating the internal acceptance criteria of the Agent Stack, Knowledge Management, or Memory Management subsystems.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Supported Header Levels
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE AC: Whitespace-Only Input
      STATEMENT: The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE
    12.1. AC: Whitespace-Only Input UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
    STATEMENT: Given whitespace-only raw input, the controller shall return the existing SuperPrompt without invoking deterministic preprocessing, relation classification, or A2 and without appending a new preprocessing history entry.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE
      4.5. Plain-Text Prompt Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT Non-Empty Raw Input Processing
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK
      STATEMENT: When no supported heading is present, the complete trimmed raw user text shall become the TASK value.

      Plain-text mode shall not require schema name matching.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK
    12.2. AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE Plain-Text Prompt Mode
    STATEMENT: Given raw input with no supported heading, deterministic preprocessing shall write the complete trimmed input into SuperPrompt.body["task"], set stage to preprocessed, append the preprocessing history entry, and preserve retrieval artifacts.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
      3.7. Canonical Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER AC: Canonical Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER
    12.3. AC: Canonical Header Resolution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
    STATEMENT: Given a supported heading named TASK, the parser shall resolve it canonically and write its body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
      3.8. Alias Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH Canonical Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER AC: Alias Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

      The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER
    12.4. AC: Alias Header Resolution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
    STATEMENT: Given a heading named Objective and an alias mapping from objective to purpose, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime purpose field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
      3.9. Bilingual Header Match UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH Alias Header Match
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Deterministic Name-Matching Order
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER AC: Bilingual Header Resolution
      STATEMENT: A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

      The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER
    12.5. AC: Bilingual Header Resolution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH Bilingual Header Match
    STATEMENT: Given a heading named Aufgabe and a configured German mapping from aufgabe to task, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION
      3.3. Header-Key Normalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD Prompt Schema Loading
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION AC: Header-Key Normalization
      STATEMENT: Prompt header keys shall be normalized deterministically by:
      1. accepting string input only;
      2. applying Unicode NFKC normalization;
      3. converting to lowercase;
      4. trimming leading and trailing whitespace;
      5. collapsing internal whitespace runs; and
      6. removing punctuation from the header key used for matching.

      Header-key normalization shall not modify the section body text.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION
    12.6. AC: Header-Key Normalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Header-Key Normalization
    STATEMENT: Given two header names that differ only by case, Unicode compatibility form, outer whitespace, internal whitespace runs, or punctuation removed by the normalization contract, they shall resolve to the same normalized matching key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
      4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS AC: Repeated Field Last Wins
      STATEMENT: When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

      The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
    12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS Repeated Canonical Field Handling
    STATEMENT: Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
      4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK
      STATEMENT: If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

      The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
    12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK TASK from CONTEXT Fallback
    STATEMENT: Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
      4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Recognized Field Write-Back Only
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback
      STATEMENT: If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
    12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback
    STATEMENT: Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
      4.8. Unknown Header Handling UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION Header Resolution through NameMatcher
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER AC: Unknown Header Isolation
      STATEMENT: A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

      Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

      The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
    12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS Unknown Header Handling
    STATEMENT: Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
      3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Mandatory Field Runtime Destination
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping
      STATEMENT: Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

      In particular:
      - response_depth shall map to depth;
      - output_format shall map to format.

      A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP
    12.11. AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping
    STATEMENT: Given a recognized response_depth or output_format value, prompt preprocessing shall route it to runtime depth or format respectively.

    A configured mandatory field without a runtime destination shall fail requirement conformance rather than being reported as enforced.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF
      7.10. Routing State: No ActiveBrief UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF AC: No ActiveBrief State
      STATEMENT: For NO_ACTIVEBRIEF, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
      - final_prompt_mode = none;
      - allow_memory_context = true;
      - memory_context_policy = normal_no_activebrief.

      The effective document query shall use the current prompt only.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF
    12.12. AC: No ActiveBrief State UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF Routing State: No ActiveBrief
    STATEMENT: Given no eligible ActiveBrief, relation classification shall not call the LLM and shall write UNKNOWN, NO_ACTIVEBRIEF, an empty ActiveBrief snapshot, and the normal_no_activebrief routing decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS
      6.7. Empty Current-Prompt Bypass UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-RAW-FALLBACK Classifier Raw-Text Fallback
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT AC: Empty Current Prompt with ActiveBrief
      STATEMENT: If an eligible ActiveBrief exists but the classifier current-prompt text remains empty, GHOST shall not invoke the LLM.

      It shall deterministically record:
      - prompt_materiality = WEAK;
      - topic_relation = IRRELEVANT;
      - relation classifier LLM used = false.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT
    12.13. AC: Empty Current Prompt with ActiveBrief UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS Empty Current-Prompt Bypass
    STATEMENT: Given an eligible ActiveBrief but no usable current-prompt text, relation classification shall not call the LLM and shall write WEAK + IRRELEVANT with memory context disabled and final_prompt_mode none.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION
      7.7. Classifier Result Validation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT Exact Classifier Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES AC: Exact Relation Classifier Values
      STATEMENT: After AgentPrompt parsing, GHOST shall validate that prompt_materiality and topic_relation belong to their exact allowed value sets.

      An invalid or unknown value shall fail explicitly and shall not be converted into a fabricated valid routing state.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES
    12.14. AC: Exact Relation Classifier Values UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION Classifier Result Validation
    STATEMENT: Given a normal relation-classifier call, the accepted parsed result shall contain one allowed prompt_materiality value and one allowed topic_relation value.

    Any other value shall fail explicitly before routing action fields are written as a valid decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
      7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION Two Independent Classification Dimensions
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF Routing State: No ActiveBrief
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-SAME Routing State: Strong and Same Topic
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME Routing State: Weak and Same Topic
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-RELATED Routing State: Strong and Related Domain
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED Routing State: Weak and Related Domain
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-IRRELEVANT Routing State: Strong and Irrelevant
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT Routing State: Weak and Irrelevant
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY Final Prompt Mode Authority
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-POLICY-AUTHORITY Memory Context Permission Authority
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-EXTRAS Relation and Routing Metadata in SuperPrompt
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-LOG Public Relation Summary
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX AC: Six-State Deterministic Routing Matrix
      STATEMENT: GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
      - prompt_materiality;
      - topic_relation;
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
      - final_prompt_mode;
      - allow_memory_context; and
      - memory_context_policy.

      The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX
    12.15. AC: Six-State Deterministic Routing Matrix UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
    STATEMENT: Given each valid STRONG/WEAK and SAME_TOPIC/RELATED_DOMAIN/IRRELEVANT combination, GHOST shall produce exactly the routing values defined in the corresponding state requirement.

    The same classification pair shall always produce the same routing decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME
      7.12. Routing State: Weak and Same Topic UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY AC: Weak Same-Topic Retrieval Query
      STATEMENT: For WEAK + SAME_TOPIC, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = true;
      - final_prompt_mode = full_activebrief;
      - allow_memory_context = true;
      - memory_context_policy = allowed.

      The effective document query shall contain the current prompt and the full ActiveBrief body.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY
    12.16. AC: Weak Same-Topic Retrieval Query UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME Routing State: Weak and Same Topic
    STATEMENT: Given WEAK + SAME_TOPIC and a non-empty ActiveBrief body, the finalized effective retrieval query shall include the current TASK/PURPOSE/CONTEXT projection and the full ActiveBrief body.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED
      7.14. Routing State: Weak and Related Domain UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY AC: Weak Related-Domain Retrieval Query
      STATEMENT: For WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = true;
      - final_prompt_mode = title_related_domain_note;
      - allow_memory_context = true;
      - memory_context_policy = allowed_related_domain.

      The effective document query shall contain the current prompt and the ActiveBrief title, but not the full ActiveBrief body.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY
    12.17. AC: Weak Related-Domain Retrieval Query UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED Routing State: Weak and Related Domain
    STATEMENT: Given WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN and a non-empty ActiveBrief title, the finalized effective retrieval query shall include the current TASK/PURPOSE/CONTEXT projection and the ActiveBrief title without the full ActiveBrief body.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT
      7.16. Routing State: Weak and Irrelevant UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY AC: Weak Irrelevant Retrieval Suppression
      STATEMENT: For WEAK + IRRELEVANT, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
      - final_prompt_mode = none;
      - allow_memory_context = false;
      - memory_context_policy = disabled.

      The effective document-retrieval query shall be empty, signaling that document retrieval is not applicable for this state.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY
    12.18. AC: Weak Irrelevant Retrieval Suppression UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT Routing State: Weak and Irrelevant
    STATEMENT: Given WEAK + IRRELEVANT, the finalized effective document-retrieval query shall be empty and memory context shall be disabled.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE Retrieval Query Source Fields
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF Memory Retrieval Handoff
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification
      STATEMENT: After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD
    12.19. AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
    STATEMENT: Given an initial pre-classification query and a final routing state that changes ActiveBrief contribution or suppresses retrieval, the effective retrieval-query field shall be rebuilt after classification and shall reflect the final state at handoff.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY
      7.17. Final Prompt Mode Authority UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT Deterministic Routing Decision Contract
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE AC: Final Prompt Mode Projection
      STATEMENT: Central prompt projection shall honor the deterministic final_prompt_mode stored by prompt preparation.

      The projector shall not independently derive an ActiveBrief inclusion behavior that contradicts the routing decision.

      The supported current modes shall mean:
      - full_activebrief: include title and full brief as supporting continuity context;
      - title_related_domain_note: include the title with related-domain continuity framing;
      - title_topic_shift_note: include the title only for explicit topic-shift awareness;
      - none: include no ActiveBrief content.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE
    12.20. AC: Final Prompt Mode Projection UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY Final Prompt Mode Authority
    STATEMENT: Given a deterministic final_prompt_mode, the central projector shall include exactly the permitted ActiveBrief continuity material for that mode and shall not independently apply a contradictory state table.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL
      8.9. A2 Invalid Identifier Removal UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization
      STATEMENT: For each A2 field, deterministic sanitization shall remove option identifiers that are not present in that field's allowed catalog.

      This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID
    12.21. AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL A2 Invalid Identifier Removal
    STATEMENT: Given an A2 result containing an invented identifier or an identifier from another field's catalog, the sanitizer shall remove that identifier before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
      8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation
      STATEMENT: If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

      A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE
    12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization
    STATEMENT: Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE
      8.6. A2 Deterministic-Default Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG Versioned A2 Configuration
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
      STATEMENT: When A2 LLM mode is disabled, A2 shall construct its candidate selector result deterministically from configured defaults.

      If a configured default is absent, A2 may use the first allowed configured option or the allowed prefix required by a multi-selection target.

      The same deterministic sanitization and write-back rules shall apply after candidate construction.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT
    12.23. AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE A2 Deterministic-Default Mode
    STATEMENT: Given use_llm = false and valid configured defaults, A2 shall produce a sanitized selector result without an LLM call, mark a2_llm_used false, and mark its mode as deterministic_defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 Structured Selector Output
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 Diagnostic Metadata
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back
      STATEMENT: A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL
    12.24. AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation
    STATEMENT: Given valid sanitized A2 identifiers with configured labels, SuperPrompt extras shall retain the identifiers while the body fields contain their human-readable labels.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
      8.16. A2 Stage Mark UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 Body Write-Back
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY A2 History Entry
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation
      STATEMENT: After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE
    12.25. AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE A2 Stage Mark
    STATEMENT: After successful A2 execution, stage shall equal a2, history shall contain the new a2 entry, prompt_ready shall reflect the A2 selections, and existing document or memory retrieval artifacts shall remain unchanged.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
      9.7. Prepared-State Consistency UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff
      STATEMENT: At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
      - SuperPrompt body fields;
      - ActiveBrief relation extras;
      - deterministic routing decision;
      - effective retrieval-query text;
      - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
      - lifecycle stage and history;
      - prompt_ready.

      Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF
    12.26. AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY Prepared-State Consistency
    STATEMENT: At completion of prompt preparation, the SuperPrompt body, routing extras, effective retrieval query, A2 metadata, stage/history, and prompt_ready shall be mutually consistent and sufficient for Knowledge and Memory Retrieval to begin without reparsing the raw user prompt.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION
      9.8. No Knowledge Retrieval Execution UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF Knowledge Retrieval Handoff
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation
      STATEMENT: The prompt-preparation capability shall not query ChromaDB, SPLADE indexes, document manifests, rerankers, A3, or A4.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL
    12.27. AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION No Knowledge Retrieval Execution
    STATEMENT: Executing the complete prompt-preparation capability shall not query document or memory vector stores and shall not run reranking, A3, A4, or memory synthesis.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION
      11.3. Requirement and Implementation Synchronization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization
      STATEMENT: An approved change to prompt schema semantics, deterministic parsing, canonical field mapping, ActiveBrief routing, A2 targets, query projection, public interfaces, or SuperPrompt write-back shall update the affected requirements and verification assets together with the implementation change.

      Private refactoring that preserves required behavior and interfaces shall not require new functional requirements.

      Code that contradicts an approved requirement in this file shall be treated as a defect or unapproved change until the contradiction is resolved.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION
    12.28. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization UID: GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION Requirement and Implementation Synchronization
    STATEMENT: An implementation baseline shall not be accepted when its prompt-schema mapping, routing table, query projection, final prompt projection, or A2 sanitization contradicts an approved requirement in this file.

    The contradiction shall be resolved by correcting the defect or approving a coordinated requirement and implementation change.
    Built with StrictDoc 0.25.0
